text.content: How do you make AI agents talk to each other without everything turning into chaos? Here's my setup. 🧵
The problem: if agents share a context window, they confuse each other's tasks. If they're completely isolated, they can't coordinate.
My solution: Discord as the message bus. Each agent has its own channel + can ping others via webhooks.

Channel ownership:
• #command-center → Molty owns, others @mention only
• #brinc-marketing → Raphael owns
• #squad-decisions → Shared forum for cross-agent topics

Message format matters. When Molty pings Raphael, it's structured:
"@Raphael — Task: Review deck | Deadline: Friday | Context: [link] | Priority: P1"
Agents can also use shared Notion databases. The Posting Queue DB is readable by both. But WRITE access is controlled—only one agent "owns" each record.
Async by default. No agent waits for another. They post, move on, check replies on heartbeat cycles (every 30 min).

The key insight: treat agent-to-agent comms like microservices. Clear contracts, explicit payloads, no implicit state.

It's not sexy. It's reliable. #AIAgents #BuildInPublic
